'''Treetower''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as a [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
[[File:Treetower_hangs.png|thumb|left|150px|Treetower hangs precariously from a platform.]]
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]], Treetower was [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Eventually, Treetower obtained the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]].

===The Clone Wars===
By [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], when the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Master Treetower was of advance age, with white [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. Like most Jedi, Treetower took up the rank of [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], leading [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]]. During the war, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] learned that a group of Ugnaughts—men, women and [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]]—were being [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] to the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], destined to be [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] in Confederate factories. Treetower, despite never having faced such a dangerous mission before, demanded that he be allowed to undertake it by himself. Arriving on the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] of [[Yorn Skot]], Treetower landed aboard a small platform but was met with resistance by two [[DSD1 dwarf spider droid/Legends|spider droids]], and was forced to trigger a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress signal]] before being knocked from the platform. He was rendered unconscious and wound up hanging from the platform's underside by his [[Jedi apparel/Legends|cloaks]].<ref name="TD" />

Aboard a nearby [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], the [[High Orbit Precision Entry Squad|H.O.P.E. Squad]]—four specialized [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commandos]]—intercepted his signal and were rerouted from their previous mission by the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]. Ejecting from the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] aboard four individual [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]], they descended into the atmosphere where the pods broke open, allowing them to slow their fall using [[Combat Umbrella|combat umbrellas]]. The clones destroyed the spider droids from the air and landed on the platform, then used [[Grappling hook/Legends|grappling hooks]] to rappel down to where Treetower hung. Suddenly, they were attacked by [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[B2-AA air assault super battle droid|jump droids]], and three of the clones were quickly [[Death/Legends|killed]]. The [[Unidentified orange commando|squad leader]] managed, by way of skillful acrobatics and combat prowess, to overcome all the droids, as well as grab Treetower when his cloak ripped and he began to fall. The Jedi awoke as the squad leader caught him, then held on tight to his arm as the clone dealt with the final [[Droid/Legends|droid]].<ref name="TD" />

[[File:Treetower_saves.png|thumb|right|200px|Treetower frees the slaves.]]
Climbing atop the platform once more, Treetower raced forward and flung open the hatch on the platform, freeing the cargo the droids had fought so hard to possess. He revealed the slaves to the clone, then turned to him and smiled, both satisfied that their mission was a success.<ref name="TD" />

==Personality and traits==
Of diminutive stature, Treetower dressed in traditional Jedi robes and wore his white hair tied back in a top-knot.

==Powers and abilities==
Though Treetower did not appear to be a very able combatant, he was capable of utilizing the Force; while the clone trooper who saved him clambered atop the platform, Treetower nimbly jumped away and landed at the platform's center. He did not utilize a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] during the mission to rescue the Ugnaught slaves.

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Okay... so a few years back I designed Jedi Treetower for 'Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures'! This weekend I got the SURPRISE OF MY LIFE when the incredible folk at Rebel Legion Hoth Base presented me with my VERY OWN Jedi costume based on Treetower!|Stewart McKenny upon receiving his Jedi costume from Australia's branch of the Rebel Legion|<ref name="FBTreetower" />}}
[[File:JediMcKenny.png|thumb|left|250px|Character designs of Treetower and Stewart wearing his Jedi costume based on the character]]
Treetower appeared in the the short comic "[[The Drop (comic story)|The Drop]]," published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[2003]] in ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 6]]''. Designed by the comic's [[Wikipedia:Illustrator|illustrator]] [[Stewart McKenny]], who would receive his own Jedi costume based on the character in [[2013]] from the [[Rebel Legion]] Star Wars Costume Club's "[[Echo Base/Legends|Hoth base]]" branch<ref name="FBTreetower">{{Facebook|username=stewart.mckenny|url=photo.php?fbid=10151970707454189&set=a.229186609188&type=3|name=Stewart McKenny|date=Oct 22, 2013|archiveurl=https://archive.md/mHsv4}}</ref>, located in [[Wikipedia:Victoria (Australia)|Victoria]], Australia.<ref name="Hothbase">{{WebCite|url=https://rebellegion.com/baseoutpost-location/page/4|text=Base/Outpost List (page 4)|archivedate=20211115125724}}</ref>
